content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 2, 1988


Mrs. Sherry L. Baum
Vice President Board of Trustees
Coast Community College District
1370 Adams Avenue
Costa Mesa, CA 92626

Re: Your Request for Information
Our File No. G-88-206

Dear Mrs. Baum:

I am in receipt of your request for two copies of the
Commission's recently adopted revision to the regulation
regarding mass mailings sent at public expense (2 Cal. Code of
Regs. Section 18901). I am forwarding the requested copies.

Your letter makes reference to "strict regulations
regarding mailing of class schedules." The regulation would
affect such mailings only if:

1. The schedules are mailed at public expense;

2. The schedules feature an elected officer; and

3. The schedules are mailed to at least 200 addressees
within the district, who have not requested the schedules, at a
time when the elected officer who is featured has filed the
documents to seek election and the election is pending.

I trust that these materials will assist you in
understanding the Commission's interpretation of Government
Code Section 89001, which provides as follows:

89001. Newsletter or Mass Mailing. No newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense by or on
behalf of any elected officer to any person residing within
the jurisdiction from which the elected officer was
elected, or to which he or she seeks election, after the
elected officer has filed either of the following:

Sherry L. Baum
June 1, 1988
Page -2-


(a) The nomination documents, as defined in Section
6489 of the Elections Code, for any local, state, or
federal office to be voted upon at an election governed by
Chapter 5 (commencing with Section 6400) of Division 6 of
the Elections Code.

(b) The last document necessary to be listed on the
ballot as a candidate for any local, state, or federal
office to be voted upon at an election not governed by
Chapter 5 (commencing with Section 6400) of Division 6 of
the Elections Code.

If you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be
reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

May 16, 1988

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Please send two copies of the Fair Political Practices Commission Regulations on Mass Mailing.
I understand that the Commission has adopted some strict regulations regarding mailing of class
schedules.

Thank you,

Mrs. Sherry L. Baum
Vice President
Board of Trustees

sections.conclusion: The regulation would affect such mailings only if: 1. The schedules are mailed at public expense; 2. The schedules feature an elected officer; and 3. The schedules are mailed to at least 200 addressees within the district, who have not requested the schedules, at a time when the elected officer who is featured has filed the documents to seek election and the election is pending.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the FPPC regulations regarding mass mailings sent at public expense, specifically regarding the mailing of class schedules by elected officers?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mass mailing regulations apply only when public expense mailings feature an elected officer, are sent to 200+ unrequested addressees within the district, and occur after the officer has filed to seek election with a pending election. Government Code Section 89001 prohibits newsletters or mass mailings sent at public expense by elected officers after filing nomination documents or ballot designation documents.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter followed by FPPC response letter. Response provides regulatory interpretation with specific conditions but lacks formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION headers. Confidence reduced due to no explicit question section in response, though intent is clear from context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the FPPC regulations regarding mass mailings sent at public expense, specifically regarding the mailing of class schedules by elected officers?

CONCLUSION: The regulation would affect such mailings only if: 1. The schedules are mailed at public expense; 2. The schedules feature an elected officer; and 3. The schedules are mailed to at least 200 addressees within the district, who have not requested the schedules, at a time when the elected officer who is featured has filed the documents to seek election and the election is pending.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to Coast Community College District's request for copies of the Commission's mass mailing regulations, clarifying that such regulations apply only to public expense mailings featuring an elected officer sent to 200+ unrequested addressees during a pending election.
